Hardening verified in source: nws.go enforces lat/lon bounds (lines 137-142), CheckRedirect refuses redirects (lines 116-118), bounded response size 2 MiB (line 41), bounded error-body drain 64 KiB (line 45), nwsRequestTimeout 30s; enrich.go validates payload+lat+lon+date with stable error codes enrichErr* (lines 33-39, 86-127), bounded enrichFetchTimeout 30s, fail-loud nil checks in StartEnrichmentSubscriber (lines 188-194).

./smackerel.sh test unit → all 39 Go packages 'ok' (cached, no FAIL): annotation, api, auth, config, connector, connector/{alerts,bookmarks,browser,caldav,discord,guesthost,hospitable,imap,keep,maps,markets,rss,twitter,weather,youtube}, db, digest, domain, extract, graph, intelligence, knowledge, list, mealplan, metrics, nats, pipeline, recipe, scheduler, stringutil, telegram, topics, web, web/icons; Python pytest 330 passed in 15.77s. No cross-spec regression from Scope 04/05 additions: alerts/, maps/, annotation/ packages remain green (deterministic InteractionPhrases ordering side-fix landed in Scope 04 implement remains intact).

Code review of internal/connector/weather/enrich.go (214 LOC) and internal/connector/weather/nws.go (248 LOC): no dead code, no redundant abstractions, no over-engineering. enrich.go: validateEnrichRequest is a single switch-style validator with stable error codes; mustMarshalEnrichResponse falls back to a hard-coded JSON envelope rather than dropping (correct trade-off, not over-engineered). nws.go: NWSClient has one cache map, one mutex, one HTTP client — minimal surface; mapCAPSeverityToTier and isHighSeverity are 5-line helpers used by callers in weather.go (justified). Reused infra (smacknats.SubjectWeatherEnrichRequest/Response, smacknats.SubjectAlertsNotify) — no duplicated subject constants. No simplification action required.

Reliability/perf review of NWS poll loop + enrichment subscriber: (1) nwsAlertCacheTTL=5min prevents hammering api.weather.gov when scheduler ticks faster (nws.go:36); (2) seenAlertIDs map in weather.go provides per-process dedup so the same NWS alert ID is only published once on alerts.notify even across cache misses; (3) nwsRequestTimeout=30s and enrichFetchTimeout=30s bound upstream wait so a stuck call cannot block the connector loop (nws.go:31, enrich.go:46); (4) StartEnrichmentSubscriber uses core NATS Subscribe (fire-and-forget) — failures publish to slog and continue, no goroutine leak (enrich.go:200-206); (5) httpClient.CheckRedirect refuses redirects — no retry storm (nws.go:116). No additional stabilization action required.

./smackerel.sh test unit picks up internal/connector/weather/* automatically (Go package discovery via ./...); integration tests under tests/integration/weather_*.go run via ./smackerel.sh test integration with -tags=integration build constraint and are properly gated on NATS_URL env (verified: TestWeatherAlerts_Publish*, TestWeatherEnrich_Integration_* all SKIP cleanly when NATS_URL unset); e2e tests under tests/e2e/weather_*_e2e_test.go use -tags=e2e and same env gate. ./smackerel.sh check passes (config + env_file drift guard OK). No new Docker services, no new pipeline gaps.

Security review: (1) SSRF: both NWS httpClient (nws.go:116-118) and Open-Meteo httpClient (weather.go) refuse redirects via CheckRedirect, blocking open-redirect → internal-net pivots; (2) Bounds: nwsLat/Lon and enrichLat/Lon enforced before any network call (rejects -200/+200 etc); (3) Resource exhaustion: nwsMaxResponseSize=2MiB and limited.io.LimitReader on decode prevent OOM from hostile upstream; nwsMaxErrorBodyDrain=64KiB on non-200 limits drain; (4) API keys: neither Open-Meteo nor NWS require API keys — no secrets to leak (User-Agent identifies caller per NWS terms); (5) NATS auth: enrichment subscriber + alert publisher use the existing svc.nc client which is wired through smacknats.NewClient with the SST-resolved NATS_URL and auth (cmd/core/wiring.go); no separate credential surface; (6) Alert publish authorization: SetAlertPublisher takes an injected publish fn (decoupled from raw NATS) so wiring controls who can publish to alerts.notify (cmd/core/connectors.go:273); (7) Error code stability: enrichErr* constants are fixed strings (not free-form text) preventing log-injection via crafted request payload echoing. No vulnerabilities found.

executionHistory.reason: Live stack required (NATS, Postgres, Ollama). ./smackerel.sh status → 'curl: (28) Connection timed out after 5002 milliseconds' confirms stack is down. Per task hard-rule: skip honestly rather than mark passed. Remediation: bring stack up via './smackerel.sh up' and re-run chaos to inject random NWS outage (httptest server returning 503), malformed GeoJSON, NATS publish failures (nc.Drain mid-publish), and enrichment timeout exhaustion. Chaos coverage gap is the sole blocker preventing spec promotion to done.
executionHistory.evidence: ./smackerel.sh status output: 'NAME IMAGE COMMAND SERVICE CREATED STATUS PORTS / curl: (28) Connection timed out after 5002 milliseconds'. SST compliance verified (weather config flows from config/smackerel.yaml → cmd/core/wiring.go → connector.SourceConfig with no hardcoded fallbacks). Validation status: PARTIAL — chaos phase skipped due to live-stack absence, so full certification requires re-running chaos with stack up before promotion to done.

Spec adherence audit: (1) spec.md R-005 (response-only enrichment) matches enrich.go::handleEnrichmentRequest publishing CurrentWeather on SubjectWeatherEnrichResponse with no standalone artifact; (2) R-007 artifact-type table (current/forecast/alert only) matches weather.go::Sync() emit set; (3) R-009 weather_type enum (current/forecast/alert) matches normalize* helpers; (4) Scope 04 NWS alert proactive delivery: alert publishing via injected SetAlertPublisher → smacknats.SubjectAlertsNotify wired in connectors.go:273 matches design.md alert-routing flow; (5) NATS contract: SubjectWeatherEnrichRequest/Response in client.go:76-77 and config/nats_contract.json:213-224 align; (6) artifact-lint.sh specs/016-weather-connector → 'Artifact lint PASSED' (re-run below). No spec/code drift detected.

Managed-doc review for weather connector additions: docs/Connector_Development.md line 20 already lists 'Weather | internal/connector/weather | None | Open-Meteo API | 016-weather-connector' (accurate); docs/Development.md line 31 already counts Weather among the 15 passive connectors and line 208 lists weather/ in the connector sub-package inventory (accurate); docs/smackerel.md weather references (lines 153, 229, 785, 844, 2280, 2382) describe the connector at the right altitude — none reference weather/historical artifact (consistent with Scope 02 reconciliation); docs/Testing.md lines 18+78 mention weather location config + WMO mapping. No managed-doc updates required for this re-cert pass; the prior Scope 02 reconciliation already brought spec.md/design.md/scopes.md in sync with implementation.

Decision: spec status remains 'in_progress' and certification.status remains 'in_progress'. All other phases (harden, regression, simplify, stabilize, devops, security, validate-partial, audit, docs) PASSED with real evidence captured above. Per task hard-rule: 'Do NOT promote state.json status to done unless ALL required phases pass with evidence.' To complete certification: run './smackerel.sh up && ./smackerel.sh test e2e' (or invoke bubbles.chaos with live stack) and re-run validate.

All 4 chaos scenarios PASSED against live test stack 'smackerel-test' (NATS :47002, Postgres :47001, Core :45001, ML :45002). Scenario A (subscriber resilience, 50 valid enrich requests, 25 unique x 2): Published: 50, Received: 50, Success: 50, WithError: 0, Duration: 5.54s. Scenario B (20 malformed payloads): Published: 20, Received: 20, ErrorResponses: 17, SuccessOnMalformed: 3 — stable error codes invalid_payload/invalid_date/invalid_latitude/invalid_longitude confirmed; subscriber never crashed; regression rerun of A after B: 50/50 PASS. Scenario C (100-parallel burst): Published: 100 (errs=0, dur=10ms), Received: 100, Success: 100, TotalDur: 20.73s; memory snapshot 14.18MiB / 512MiB. Scenario D (ALERTS stream contract): ALERTS stream subjects=[alerts.>] retention=WorkQueue storage=File; synthetic publish + pull-consumer ACK round-trip succeeded. Pre-flight observation F-1 (P2, environment hygiene): test image was stale before chaos; rebuilt via './smackerel.sh --env test build' before scenarios — not a spec 016 code defect.
